name: Mango Chutney (the Book of Hot & Spicy Foods)
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice mangos in half by cutting lengthwise close to the seeds on either side.
2. Peel and cut flesh into 1/8"-thick slices. Also cut away as much mango flesh as possible from around pits, without including any fibrous parts of the pits.
3. Heat oil in a large saucepan.
4. Add mangos, ginger, garlic, salt, chili powder, cumin, and fenugreek to the hot oil. Cook gently for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Stir in vinegar, raisins, lemon juice, and sugar. Cook slowly until the sugar dissolves.
6. Bring to a boil and simmer, uncovered, for 35-40 minutes or until the liquid thickens and becomes syrupy, and the mangos become translucent, stirring frequently.
7. Meanwhile, wash and rinse pint jars in hot soapy water; rinse again. Keep hot until needed.
8. Prepare lids according to manufacturer's directions.
9. Ladle hot chutney into 1 jar at a time, leaving 1/4 inch headspace.
10. Release trapped air by tapping the jar lightly on the counter.
11. Wipe rim of the jar with a clean damp cloth.
12. Attach lid and place in canner.
13. Fill and close remaining jars.
14. Process for 10 minutes in a boiling-water bath.
15. To serve, garnish with parsley sprig and lemon peel if desired.
Makes 2-1/2 pounds.
